headline: Forced labour indicators found in 3 glove manufacturing plants
context: A 2025 Department of Labour investigation found forced labour indicators in 3 major glove manufacturing plants employing over 8,000 migrant workers.

cards.big: Forced labour indicators found in 3 glove factories employing 8,000 workers
cards.sub: Debt bondage and passport confiscation documented by labour inspectors

cards.big: Anti-Trafficking in Persons Act 2007 criminalises forced labour with 15-year penalty
cards.sub: Only 12 forced labour prosecutions completed between 2020 and 2024

cards.big: ILO Forced Labour Protocol ratified by Malaysia in 2022
cards.sub: Ratification has not translated to significant increase in enforcement actions

cards.big: Malaysian glove industry worth RM18 billion relies on 65% migrant workforce
cards.sub: US CBP issued WROs against 2 Malaysian glove makers between 2020-2022

cards.big: An RM18 billion industry built on forced labour indicators is an economic house of cards
MITI launched ethical manufacturing certification scheme in February 2025
